data.title: Jardine Matheson Cancels 18,000 Shares After Market Buyback
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jardine Matheson Holdings ( (GB:JAR) ) has provided an update.

Jardine Matheson Holdings has repurchased 18,000 of its ordinary shares on 31 March 2026 at a price of US$71.6719 per share, with the company confirming that these shares will be cancelled. Following the transaction, the group’s issued share capital stands at 293,147,530 ordinary shares, each carrying one vote, a figure shareholders may use to assess disclosure thresholds for changes in their holdings under applicable transparency rules.

The company stated that it holds no treasury shares, meaning all issued shares carry voting rights, which can slightly enhance proportional ownership for remaining investors after the cancellation. This repurchase and cancellation marginally reduces the share count, potentially supporting earnings per share metrics and reflecting ongoing capital management, while clarifying the total voting rights available in the market.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:JAR) stock is a Buy with a $94.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Jardine Matheson Holdings stock, see the GB:JAR Stock Forecast page.

More about Jardine Matheson Holdings

Jardine Matheson Holdings is a diversified conglomerate with interests spanning sectors such as retail, property, automotive, and other commercial services across Asia and beyond. The group focuses on long-term investments in market-leading businesses in its key regional markets, positioning itself as a core player in consumer and infrastructural growth.

Average Trading Volume: 807

Tirupati Graphite Plc ( (GB:TGR) ) just unveiled an update.

Tirupati Graphite plc has reported that, as of 31 March 2026, its issued share capital comprises 762,447,924 ordinary shares of £0.01 each, with one voting right per share and no shares held in treasury. This confirms that the total number of voting rights in the company stands at 762,447,924, a key reference figure shareholders must use when assessing whether they need to disclose holdings or changes in their ownership under the FCA’s transparency rules.

The clarification of total voting rights provides investors and regulators with an updated denominator for calculating significant shareholdings in Tirupati Graphite. By formalising this capital structure information, the company supports compliance with U.K. disclosure requirements and enhances transparency for existing and prospective shareholders tracking their reporting obligations.

Tirupati Graphite plc operates in the graphite sector, focusing on the production and development of graphite materials used in industrial, energy storage, and other technology applications. data.title: INWIT Calls 2026 Shareholders’ Meeting to Approve 2025 Accounts and Cancel Treasury Shares
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Inwit ( (IT:INW) ) is now available.

INWIT has convened an ordinary and extraordinary shareholders’ meeting for 30 April 2026 to approve the 2025 financial statements, decide on profit allocation, vote on the 2026 remuneration policy and 2025 pay report, and adopt a new 2026–2030 long‑term share‑based incentive plan. Shareholders will also appoint a new director and vote on the cancellation of treasury shares without reducing share capital, with participation allowed exclusively via a designated representative, supported by detailed rules for agenda additions, advance proposals, questions, remote voting and proxy procedures.

The meeting will be held through Computershare as the sole designated representative, reflecting continued reliance on remote corporate governance mechanisms under Italian emergency legislation extended into 2026. INWIT has outlined clear timelines and channels for shareholders to submit proposals, ask questions and vote electronically, underscoring an effort to maintain shareholder engagement and transparency while progressing governance, capital structure optimisation and long‑term incentive alignment with management.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:INW) stock is a Hold with a EUR7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Inwit stock, see the IT:INW Stock Forecast page.

More about Inwit

Infrastrutture Wireless Italiane S.p.A. (INWIT) operates in the Italian telecommunications infrastructure sector, focusing on wireless towers and related services that support mobile network operators. The company manages shared passive infrastructure to enable efficient deployment of mobile and broadband connectivity across Italy, positioning itself as a key player in the national digital and telecoms ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 5,014,303

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: €6.14B

data.title: Silkwave Inc Updates Nomination Committee Terms to Bolster Governance
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from CMMB Vision Holdings ( (HK:0471) ).

Silkwave Inc has amended and restated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee with effect from 31 March 2026, clarifying its mandate to review board composition and recommend procedures and criteria for appointing directors. The document sets out rules on membership, meeting frequency, quorum, voting, and administrative procedures to strengthen governance and align with Hong Kong Listing Rules and other regulatory standards.

The Nomination Committee must comprise at least three directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and will be chaired by either the board chairman or an independent non-executive director. Meetings are required at least annually or as needed, with detailed provisions on notice, participation by electronic means, written resolutions, and restrictions on members voting on matters concerning their own appointments to reinforce transparency and independence in board nomination processes.

An announcement from Sacgasco ( (AU:AOK) ) is now available.

Australian Oil Company Limited has released its 2025 Corporate Governance Statement, outlining the governance practices applied across the group for the 2025 financial year. The company affirms compliance with the fourth edition of the ASX Corporate Governance Principles and Recommendations and positions its framework as serving the best interests of shareholders.

The board’s responsibilities include setting long-term strategic goals, overseeing financial performance, approving major transactions and ensuring robust risk management and disclosure controls. It also emphasises structured processes for appointing and evaluating directors, with defined terms, regular retirement and re-election cycles, and a focus on diversity and relevant skills to support effective oversight and leadership.

Solis Minerals has lodged its updated corporate governance statement for the year ended 31 December 2025, confirming that the document is current as of that date and has been approved by the board. An announcement from Puma VCT 13 PLC ( (GB:PU13) ) is now available.

Puma VCT 13 plc has admitted a further 5,186,001 ordinary shares of £0.0005 each to trading on the London Stock Exchange Main Market, following a recent subscription offer to investors. The new shares, which are fully fungible with the existing class, bring the total number of ordinary shares in issue to 200,204,667, modestly expanding the trust’s capital base and potentially enhancing liquidity for shareholders.

The admission, effective 31 March 2026 and conducted under the framework of the Public Offers and Admissions to Trading Regulations 2024, reflects continued investor demand for the company’s venture capital trust offering. By increasing the volume of listed securities, Puma VCT 13 may strengthen its market presence and support ongoing investment activities in line with its existing prospectus dated 24 September 2025.

The latest update is out from XP Power ( (GB:XPP) ).

XP Power has reported director dealings in its ordinary shares following the exercise of share options by senior executives. Chief executive Gavin Griggs exercised options over 4,015 shares at £0.01 each and sold 1,895 shares on the London Stock Exchange to cover associated tax liabilities.

Executive vice president for Asia, Andy Sng, also exercised options over 1,244 shares at £0.01 under the company’s long-term incentive and restricted share plans. The transactions, which were conducted in line with UK Market Abuse Regulation disclosure requirements, reflect ongoing use of equity-based remuneration across XP Power’s leadership team.
